title: Work Forms - Customize Forms (CSS/HTML)
excerpt:
I would like to propose the integration of custom CSS (and/or HTML) capabilities, allowing for more personalized and brand-aligned styling within the WorkForms. 
Furthermore, the option to incorporate enhanced logo or ic…

posts.author: JSDixon (Scott Dixon )
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to propose the integration of custom CSS (and/or HTML) capabilities, allowing for more personalized and brand-aligned styling within the WorkForms.

Furthermore, the option to incorporate enhanced logo or icon imagery on forms would significantly improve the visual appeal and brand representation over the very small icon size that is available today.

posts.author: JakeHive (Jake D.)
posts.cookedHtml: I second this, and I was surprised to not find this functionality already included. Even just a simple text box to allow for adding custom css would go a long way.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @JSDixon @JakeHive

Nachliel from Easyapps here

Our Monday form builder app, Easyform, supports styling the form to better match your brand by allowing you to customize many aspects of the form. For example

  • Set the colors of all the different elements of the form,
  • HTML support in the form description as well as the question’s description
  • Set your logo with a URL or an SVG and adjust its size

In addition to that, we offer:

  • Connected Board support:
    • Limiting selection to a single item.
    • Displaying items from multiple connected boards.
    • Limiting select options to items from specific boards where a column is connected to multiple boards.
    • Pro plan feature - item default values support - set default values for connected board columns hidden in the form.
  • Upload files to your board’s file columns, with support for:
    • Allowing only specific file types, such as images or pdf.
    • Limiting the number of files that can be uploaded.
    • Setting a maximum for file size.
    • Manually retrying uploads if some or all of them fail.
  • Set default values for the response item’s hidden group, status, connected board, and dropdown columns. To do so, select Item default values from the Settings menu.
  • Add the item’s group as just another field to the form.
  • Control the page’s top margin with the ability to set different values for desktop and mobile. To do so, select Page from the Settings menu.

If you’re interested in creating styled forms that better match your brand and embed perfectly on your web page, you can check out our Monday.com app Easyform and our extensive documentation site.

You can set up a free demo here or contact us at support@easyappshq.com.

Here is an example of our form in action:

I hope this helps,

Nachliel

posts.author: daniel.jarolim (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: +one here, this would be useful for our team.

posts.author: SJarboe (Sean)
posts.cookedHtml:
Nachliel from Easyapps:

and by allowing you to customize many aspects of the form. For example

  • Set the colors of all the different elements of the form,
  • HTML support in the form description as well as the question’s description
  • Set your logo with a URL or an SVG and adjust its size

100% agree. We purchased the HIPAA compliant CRM with the allusion that we could embed and customize the form to our liking. THat is not the case. A javascript embed and CSS styling option should be available. iFrames are horrible.

posts.author: normanb (Norman Bontilao)
posts.cookedHtml: Apologies for reopening an old discussion, but we also need this.

posts.author: mondayuser6 (Jim)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% agree. The logo for the form is miniscule. We are having to link to the form instead of embedding on our site because of how the embedding messes with scrolling, especially on mobile. So, we’re opening the form in a new tab. With that, the logo we uploaded is comically small. Would love to be able to do something about that, even with CSS.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mondayuser6

As mention above, Easyform was build to handle this specific issue of embedding forms in websites. Regrading logos, we allow you to size it from 8px and up with no limit.

You can checkout the documentation to are logo sizing here.

You can set up a free demo here or contact us at support@easyappshq.com.

Here is an example of our form in action:

posts.author: snicho (Stephen Nicholson)
posts.cookedHtml: Being able to set CSS values for forms would allow greater use of monday forms, particularly allowing greater adoption for website integration, instead of having to turn to 3rd party solutions.
